100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 22, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, focusing on disarmament discussions amid rising tensions in Europe. In the United States, the Supreme Court upheld the constitutionality of the National Industrial Recovery Act, a key element of President Roosevelt's New Deal. Meanwhile, British coal miners continued their strike, drawing attention to labor disputes and economic challenges facing the country.
Updated: Monday, June 22, 2026 at 11:20:13 AM CDT
